data.title: SMCI Stock Plunges as 3 Employees Indicted for Smuggling Billions in Servers to China
data.description:
Shares of Super Micro Computer (SMCI) plunged nearly 12% in after-hours trading after a U.S. indictment accused three employees of smuggling billions in Nvidia (NVDA)-based servers to China. SMCI has placed two employees on leave and fired a contractor upon learning of their role in a scheme to evade U.S. export controls and sell high-performance AI servers in the region. This comes amid escalating U.S. restrictions on AI chip exports to China, aimed at limiting advanced computing capabilities due to national security concerns.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Super Micro is not named as a defendant in the complaint, and is referenced only as a "U.S. manufacturer." The indictment alleges that the employees instructed executives at a Southeast Asian company to order Super Micro servers equipped with specific Nvidia GPUs (graphics processing units). The firm then repackaged and shipped them to China.

From 2024 to 2025, this channel generated $2.5 billion in sales for the server maker, including $510 million from late April to mid-May 2025 routed to China without required U.S. licenses for Nvidia GPU servers.

Details of the Three Employees

  • Yih-Shyan "Wally" Liaw – SMCI co-founder, senior VP of business development and board member.
  • Ruei-Tsan "Steven" Chang – Sales Manager in Taiwan
  • Ting-Wei "Willy" Sun – Contractor

According to the Department of Justice (DOJ), Liaw and Sun were arrested on Thursday and are slated to appear in the Northern District of California, while Chang, a Taiwanese citizen, remains at large.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

How the Trio Allegedly Conspired

The Southeast Asian middleman created fake documentation claiming it would use the servers domestically. It then enlisted another firm to repackage and conceal them before shipping to China.

The defendants deceived SMCI's compliance team by staging "dummy" servers in storage, while the real ones had already shipped. They expedited shipment approvals and even used dummies during a visit from a U.S. export officer.

Chang reportedly blocked auditors from data centers where servers were supposedly stored (but had already been sent to China) and arranged a "friendly" auditor to rubber-stamp paperwork. Notably, Super Micro's previous auditor, Ernst & Young, resigned in 2024 over financial reporting issues; the company later hired BDO.

Super Micro's Response

In a statement, SMCI said the individuals' alleged actions violated the company's policies and controls designed to prevent evasion of U.S. export laws. Super Micro stressed its strong compliance program and full commitment to U.S. regulations.

Is SMCI Stock a Buy Right Now?

On TipRanks, SMCI stock has a Hold consensus rating based on two Buys, six Holds, and two Sell ratings. The average Super Micro Computer price target of $38.89 implies 26.3% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, SMCI shares have fallen 21.3%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Tesla (TSLA) Explores $2.9B Solar Equipment Deal in China – Is Musk Building an Energy Empire?
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) is reportedly in talks to purchase about $2.9 billion in solar manufacturing equipment from Chinese suppliers, including Suzhou Maxwell Technologies. According to Reuters, other potential suppliers include Shenzhen S.C. New Energy Technology and Laplace Renewable Energy Technology. The move comes as Elon Musk aims to expand U.S. solar production by 100 gigawatts, signaling a major push into clean energy alongside EVs and a step toward building a large energy empire.

data.title: '#1 Pick for 2026,' Says Top Analyst About Amazon Stock (AMZN)
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) remains a top pick for Evercore ISI, with the firm reiterating its bullish view after a deeper look at the company's cloud unit Amazon Web Services (AWS) growth and margins. Top analyst Mark Mahaney maintained an Outperform rating on the stock with a $285 price target, implying about 37% upside from current levels. He called Amazon his "#1 large-cap long idea for 2026."

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AWS Growth and Margins Support the View

Evercore raised its estimates following a detailed review of AWS. The firm now expects AWS revenue to reach $163 billion in 2026, up 27% year-over-year, and $214 billion in 2027, with 31% growth. It also sees AWS operating margins at 34% in 2026 and 32% in 2027.

Along with this, Evercore increased its overall estimates for Amazon. Revenue and operating income forecasts were raised by 2–3% and now sit about 4–5% above Street expectations.

Why Evercore Calls Amazon a Top Pick

Mahaney sees several reasons for the positive view.

He points to Amazon's current valuation, noting the stock is trading near a three-year low on a P/E basis, which he views as attractive. He also expects a possible shift this year, with faster revenue growth and margin expansion.

In addition, Evercore sees upside from newer initiatives such as Project Leo and Perishable Checkout, which could start contributing more meaningfully in 2026.

Capex Remains a Key Watch Point

Spending remains the main concern. Evercore expects Amazon's capex to rise to around $250 billion by 2027, which could weigh on near-term free cash flow.

The firm estimates about $10 billion in free cash flow losses in both 2026 and 2027. It also noted that capex intensity could peak in 2026, though there is a chance it extends into 2027.

Even so, Evercore believes much of this risk is already reflected in the stock and says the overall risk-reward remains favorable.

Is Amazon a Buy, Sell, or Hold? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on AMZN stock based on 40 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AMZN price target of $280.00 per share implies 34.13% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market Review Today: SPY, QQQ Stumble as Iran Escalates War; U.S. Weighs Options to Cool Oil Prices
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed lower on Thursday as heightened tensions across the Middle East threatened to further disrupt the oil supply chain and fuel higher inflation.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • Iran launched attacks on Saudi Aramco's Samref refinery, Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City, and Kuwait Petroleum Corporation's Mina al-Ahmadi and Mina Abdullah refineries as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed.
  • Brent crude futures (BZ), the international benchmark for oil, reversed gains after the U.S. green-lighted the sale of Russian crude oil and petroleum products loaded on vessels as of March 12.
  • The U.S. is considering lifting sanctions on Iranian oil and initiating a second Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) release. Removing the sanctions would allow oil to "flow into Malaysia, Singapore, Indonesia, Japan, India — who have been good actors in this," said Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
  • JPMorgan lowered its year-end S&P 500 (SPX) price target to 7,200 from 7,500, citing recession risks from rising oil prices. The firm's target is well below the average Wall Street estimate of 7,600.
  • Micron (MU) closed lower, despite reporting a double beat on its fiscal second-quarter revenue and adjusted EPS and raising guidance.
  • Tesla (TSLA) faced pressure after the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) upgraded its investigation into the company's Full Self-Driving (FSD) service.
  • Goldman Sachs (GS) and JPMorgan Chase (JPM) launched instruments enabling hedge funds to bet against the private credit market.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Energy was the best-performing sector for a third consecutive day as the oil supply chain in the Middle East faces new threats from Iran. With higher oil prices, producers and refiners stand to benefit from higher revenues and stronger profit margins.

Trade QQQ with leverage

Several stocks led the energy sector's gains, including:

  • Baker Hughes (BKR)
  • SLB (SLB)
  • APA (APA)
  • Phillips 66 (PSX)
  • Targa Resources (TRGP)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, materials was the worst-performing sector due to inflation risks from higher energy prices, as rising input and transportation costs could pressure margins for companies across the sector. In addition, inflation fears have lowered rate cut odds, weighing on commodity producers and mining companies.

Notable materials stocks trading lower include:

  • Newmont Mining (NEM)
  • Packaging Corp of America (PKG)
  • Ecolab (ECL)
  • PPG Industries (PPG)
  • Sherwin-Williams (SHW)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: NVDA, AMZN: Nvidia, Amazon Stocks Gain after 1 Million AI Chip Deal
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is deepening its lead in AI with a massive new deal. The company will sell 1 million AI chips to Amazon's (AMZN) cloud unit, Amazon Web Services (AWS), in one of its biggest agreements yet. Following the news, both Nvidia and Amazon shares inched higher in after-hours trading on Thursday.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An Nvidia executive confirmed that shipments will begin this year and continue through 2027. The scale of the deal stands out. Supplying 1 million chips to a single customer shows just how strong demand for AI hardware has become.

Amazon Adds Nvidia Hardware across Its AI Stack

The deal goes beyond the 1 million GPUs. Amazon is buying a mix of Nvidia's latest AI tech to support its growing cloud business.

  • Next-generation chips: AWS will deploy Nvidia's Blackwell chips and is also expected to adopt its future Rubin chips.
  • Networking equipment: AWS will add Nvidia's ConnectX and Spectrum-X gear to its data centers, alongside its own systems.
  • AI inference chips: Amazon plans to use a combination of several Nvidia chips to handle inference, where AI systems generate responses and perform tasks for users.

AI Chip Demand Remains Strong

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said during GTC that AI chips such as Blackwell and Rubin could represent a $1 trillion market opportunity by 2027. This deal aligns with that view and reflects continued demand from large cloud providers.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

Amazon already develops its own AI chips, including Trainium2. However, it is still purchasing Nvidia's hardware for more advanced and demanding AI workloads. This shows that AWS continues to rely on Nvidia's technology alongside its in-house chips.

Overall, the deal indicates that spending on AI infrastructure remains strong and continues to expand, especially among major cloud companies.

Which Stock Looks Better, According to Analysts?

Using TipRanks' comparison tool, Nvidia and Amazon both show strong analyst support. Nvidia has a Strong Buy rating, with an average price target of about $274, suggesting roughly 53% upside. Amazon also has a Strong Buy rating, with a price target near $280, implying about 34% upside.

In comparison, Nvidia offers higher potential upside, while both stocks are viewed positively by analysts.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Bitcoin Struggles Near $70,000 as "Quadruple Witching" and Global Tensions Weigh on Markets
data.description:
Bitcoin  (BTC-USD) dipped below the $70,000 mark on Thursday, pulling back into a familiar trading range after briefly reaching highs above $76,000. This slide happens as investors face a mix of high oil prices, conflict in the Middle East, and a massive derivatives event in traditional finance known as "quadruple witching." While the price action looks shaky, some analysts believe a specific chart pattern could still trigger a quick rebound.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures Selling and Stalling U.S. Demand Drive Prices Down

The recent drop aligns with a shift in how Bitcoin is being traded. Data shows that selling pressure is currently coming more from professional futures traders than from people buying the actual coins on exchanges. This is visible in the Coinbase premium gap, which turned negative, suggesting that demand from U.S. investors has cooled off.

Crypto analyst IT Tech pointed out a clear imbalance, noting that while spot selling fell by about $40 million, selling in the perpetual futures market dropped by a much larger $506.75 million. This indicates that traders using borrowed money (leverage) are the ones driving the current downward trend.

Historical Fractals Hint at Potential Rebound for BTC

Despite the drop, some traders see a bullish fractal forming. This is a pattern that looks like a previous market move. Specifically, Bitcoin is mimicking a correction from early March where the price fell to trap shorts before reversing higher.

If Bitcoin can quickly reclaim the $70,000 level, it may open a path back toward $76,000. However, the setup is time-sensitive. A drop below $68,300 could shift the focus toward lower support levels at $65,000 or even $62,000.

"Quadruple Witching" Adds to Market Stress

Adding to the uncertainty is a major event in traditional markets called "quadruple witching" on Friday. This happens four times a year when trillions of dollars in various stock and index derivatives expire at once. In 2025, Bitcoin often saw flat performance on these days followed by a week or two of price drops.

Cole Kennelly, CEO of Volmex Finance, warned that "quadruple witching could trigger a spike in cross-asset volatility as large derivatives positions expire." This volatility often spills over from the stock market into crypto.

Outside of the charts, the war in Iran and rising energy costs are making investors nervous. With oil prices swinging toward $100 a barrel, there are fears that central banks will keep interest rates high. Wintermute trader Bryan Tan suggested that "being flat is a strong position" right now. He recommends that investors keep "dry powder" (cash) ready until the market shows a clearer direction.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin is sitting at $70,358.18.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Unilever Considers Major Food Spinoff as McCormick Merger Talks Surface
data.description:
Unilever (UL) (GB:ULVR), the global giant behind Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr soup, is reportedly in talks to merge its food division with spice maker McCormick & Co (MKC). This potential deal, first reported by the Wall Street Journal, would be a massive shift for the consumer goods industry. If successful, the transaction could be valued at tens of billions of dollars and may be announced by the end of the month.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Reverse Morris Trust Structure Targets Tax Savings

To make the deal as profitable as possible, the companies are looking at a specialized system called a Reverse Morris Trust. This is a type of merger that allows a parent company like Unilever to spin off a part of its business without paying a heavy tax bill. In this case, Unilever would first turn its food division into a separate company and then merge that new company with McCormick. For the tax benefits to work, Unilever's current shareholders would need to own more than 50% of the newly combined food and spice company.

Unilever Shifts toward Beauty and Health

The move away from food is part of a larger plan by Unilever's CEO, Fernando Fernandez, to focus on "Beauty, Wellbeing, and Personal Care." Over the last year, Unilever has already sold off several other food brands, including The Vegetarian Butcher and its global spreads division. A focus on brands like Dove and Vaseline helps the company reach younger shoppers who spend more on skincare and wellness products. These categories often have higher profit margins and faster growth than traditional grocery items.

Food Giants Struggle with Changing Consumer Habits

Many large food companies, including Unilever and its rival Nestlé (NSRGF), are currently struggling to grow. This is because shoppers are feeling the pinch of high prices and are switching to cheaper store-brand versions of mayonnaise and spices.

Additionally, the rising popularity of GLP-1 weight-loss drugs has created a new threat. Since these drugs cause people to eat less, food companies are worried that total sales will continue to drop. A merger with McCormick would allow Unilever's food brands to combine forces with a market leader in seasonings to stay competitive.

Is Unilever a Good Stock to Buy?

On Wall Street, Unilever (GB:ULVR) currently has a Hold consensus rating. This is based on 11 analyst ratings gathered over the past three months, consisting of four Buy, four Hold, and three Sell recommendations.

The average 12-month price target for Unilever stock is 5,229.55 p, which suggests a 14.33% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Morgan Stanley Has Added This Software Stock to Its 'Top Pick' List. Hint: It's Tax Season
data.description:
Wall Street investment bank Morgan Stanley (MS) has named Intuit (INTU), the software company behind TurboTax, to its "Top Pick" list.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Keith Weiss, a top five-star rated analyst, called Intuit a "durable compounder at a discount." In a note to clients, the Morgan Stanley analyst writes that it is "making Intuit our Top Pick as valuation screens attractive," adding that it sees "a path to top-line acceleration" for the company, whose other software products include QuickBooks for accounting and Credit Karma for personal finance.

Weiss argues that INTU stock offers an attractive entry point to investors with the share price down 28% in 2026 and trading at about 20 times this year's earnings estimates. He adds that despite getting caught up in fears about AI's disruption of the software space, Intuit still offers long-term growth potential.

Intuit's Strong Numbers

Morgan Stanley was impressed with Intuit's most recent financial results, noting that the company reported 17% total revenue growth, 18% growth in Global Business Solutions, 21% Online Ecosystem growth, and approximately 40% growth in the its Intuit Enterprise Suite.

Weiss adds that Intuit's popular TurboTax software product is growing at about 12% a year even with Internal Revenue Service (IRS) tax filings tracking five points below prior-year levels. Morgan Stanley has a Buy rating and $580 price target on INTU stock, which is nearly 30% higher than where the shares currently trade.

Is INTU Stock a Buy?

Intuit's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 23 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 19 Buy and four Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average INTU price target of $582.82 implies 28.76% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Analyst Says XRP Price Can Hit $153 by 'Cycle 5 on Fibonacci Roadmap'
data.description:
XRP (XRP-USD) is at the center of an ambitious new price prediction that sees the token eventually climbing past the $100 mark. Crypto analyst TARA recently shared a five-step "Fibonacci roadmap" based on long-term market cycles. While the token currently trades near $1.52, this technical analysis suggests that XRP could eventually reach a peak of $153 after moving through several years of growth and pullbacks.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fibonacci Levels Map Out Five Major Price Peaks

The new forecast uses a math-based tool called Fibonacci extensions to predict where the price might stop during future rallies. According to TARA, the first cycle is already finished, with XRP hitting a high of $3.65 in mid-2025. Using that high point as a starting guide, the analyst predicts the next four cycles will hit the following peaks:

  • Cycle 2: $8.68
  • Cycle 3: $22.50
  • Cycle 4: $59.00
  • Cycle 5: $153.00

Triple-Digit Target Requires Multiple Growth Cycles

Investors hoping for a quick move to $100 might need to be patient. The analysis shows that XRP does not reach triple digits in the next one or two rallies. Instead, the $100 level only becomes a reality during "Cycle 5." The analyst did not give a specific calendar date for this, explaining that the journey will take several years. Each big move up is expected to be followed by a correction, where the price drops back down before the next cycle begins.

Massive XRP Price Predictions Create a Debate on Realism

For XRP to reach $153, it would need to grow by more than 10,000% from today. This has sparked a big debate. Some believe it is possible if XRP becomes the main tool for global bank transfers. However, others point out that at $100 per token, XRP would be worth trillions of dollars. This would make the XRP network more valuable than the biggest companies in the world, like Apple (AAPL) or Microsoft (MSFT). Even Ripple's own leadership has expressed doubt that such a high price could happen anytime soon.

At the time of writing, XRP's price is sitting at $1.4539.
data.author: Annika Masrani
